
```{r}
library(devtools)
library(testthat)

devtools::document()
devtools::test()
devtools::check()
```

```{r}
library(brfssTools)

# 1. Run the audit and save it (so you don't have to re-run)
vintage <- c(2012:2024)
brfss_download(vintage)
cw <- brfss_crosswalk(dataset = "National", year = vintage) 
audit <- brfss_crosswalk_audit(cw)
saveRDS(audit, "~/audit_results.rds")

# 2. See how big the problem is
audit |> dplyr::count(needs_action)

# 3. Look at the unique raw_var_names that fail. Group similar ones.
audit |>
  dplyr::filter(needs_action) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE)

audit |>
  dplyr::filter(needs_action) |>
  dplyr::count(raw_var_name, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 50)

# 4. Decide retag rules. Most are "yes, retag to OR." A few might be
#    "no, leave alone — that's a National variable that got renamed in
#    that year." A few might be "delete, this row was a mistake."

# 5. Edit concept_map_brfss.csv to reflect those decisions. This can be:
#    - a single dplyr pipeline that overwrites source for matching rows
#    - or hand-editing the CSV in Excel for a few hundred rows
#    - or whatever feels right

# 6. Save the file back
readr::write_csv(cm_updated,
                 "inst/extdata/concept_map_brfss.csv", na = "")

# 7. Rebuild package, run tests, commit. Done.
```
